entry.title: SAP MY Inbox Issue with SAP Fiori launchpad multiple systems ( S4 ECC and CRM )
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Need your expert advice here please -

Background:

  • We  have one MyInbox and processes workflows initiated in CRM as well as S4.
  • CRM System Alias was used to enable one MyInbox for both the systems.
  • Realization today that a manager must have an ID in both the systems or MyInbox has errors.
  • We have many managers who do not have a CRM id
  • Managers are not real SAP users but they receive mail with a link which navigates them to MyInbox for approvals.

Plans:

  • Disable CRM Alias to allow Managers to approve S4 workflows
  • Copy MyInbox so we will have two MyInboxes (one for S4 workflows and one for CRM workflows)

Where we are:

  • CRM Alias has been disabled
  • We are having challenges creating a secondary MyInbox for CRM workflows

What is needed:

  • Requesting your help in how to create second CRM MyInBox  or suggest alternatives for CRM workflows. 

Thanks & Best Regards,

Sunit

entry.title: Re: TRUNCATE TABLE PARTITION takes hours when deleting an...
entry.content:
A possible explanation, if the table has global index(s), is that TRUNCATE TABLE <table-name> PARTITION <partition-name> causes all global indexes to be rebuilt, even if the target partition is empty.  CR 829608 was opened for this a few days ago; there isn't yet a KBA for it.

A debugging approach for finding out what a process is doing is to use "dbcc stacktrace(spid)" to find out what internal functions are currently being executed.  Run it multiple times for a long-running process to get a profile of what functions are involved.  The function names are sometimes cryptic, but usually some sense of what is going on can be extracted from them.  If the top of the stack shows function names including "idx" on "indx", for instance, would point towards index building, while functions names including "oam" or "gam" would indicate ASE was searching through OAM or GAM structures to find extents belonging to the object.

To use dbcc stackstrace, first use sp_who to find the spid of the process performing the TRUNCATE, then substitute in that spid in

set switch on 3604 -- causes internal dbcc command outputs to be sent to client
go
dbcc stacktrace (spid)
go

Cheers,
-bret

entry.title: Automatic Schema Assignment for Vendor Types in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I need assistance with configuring automatic schema assignment during vendor creation in SAP S/4HANA. My client has two types of vendors: Local and Foreigner. They want the system to automatically assign the appropriate schema based on the vendor type selected.

Specifically, when creating a Foreigner vendor and selecting the Foreigner vendor group, the system should automatically fill the schema field with 'ZF'. Conversely, when creating a Local vendor and selecting the Local vendor group, the system should automatically fill the schema field with 'ZL'.

From my understanding, this functionality is not available in the standard configuration. Could you please guide me on how to achieve this requirement? Any advice on configuration or customisation steps would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Configure the Employee Central Benefit Type - Reimbursements; for Beginners Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

I would like to share my wonderful experience towards the “Global Employee Benefits” to the Beginners of Employee Central Consultant who has passionate on the Benefits Topic.

Most of them will not get the opportunity to know about the Benefits Topic/Product Features and they will not be in the comfortable zone to discuss the features in the Implementation Project > Specifically in the Fit to Standard Workshop.

The idea here is to enable the Consultant to know the basic overview of the “Benefit” topic and start discussing the same with Customer (in the Project to make use of this module and provide the extended user experience).

It is highly recommended to suggest the Customer to use the SAP SuccessFactors Best Practices and Approaches to strengthen the HR Process in the Organization.

The following are the Benefit Types in SuccessFactors.

  1. Allowance
  2. Reimbursement
  3. Pension/Savings/Retirement Plan
  4. Insurance
  5. Wallet
  6. Deductible Allowance
  7. Savings Plan
  8. Others

Most of the countries extensively can use the “Reimbursement/Claim and Insurance Types since it has the features to automate End-to-End Business Process for the customers.

As a beginner, you must talk about the benefits module and how this can be helpful for the HR Business Process, how they can strengthen HR Process and automate the claim process with end-to-end process without manual intervention.

Let us start with the easy one (Reimbursement Benefit Type), How to Configure the Business Process, How to Configure the Simple Business Rule and Enable the Role Based Permissions.

Business Requirements:

Our customer is having the following requirements / cases.

  • Air Ticket Reimbursement (this is used as a Sample Cases to share the look and feel of the configurations and understand the configurations steps)
  • 2 Level of Approval Process (Workflow)
  • Restriction or Validations by Using the Business Rule
  • Custom UI Screen High level Screen Shot
  • User Experience

Here, we are going to see the step-by-step configuration process and how to enable this functionality to Business User and Employees (Including the Workflow and Business Rules)

Section 1: Workflow and Business Rule Configuration

 

Note: You can add the validations based on the business needs. Here I have restricted with claim ID.

Note: There are two level of approvals been configured, this needs to be deployed into the benefits object (Under Validate and Save Rules respectively) as per the below screen shots.

Section 2: Step-by-Step Configuration Process

Goto > Benefit Admin Overview

Step 1 & Step 2 Specify the Benefit Type and General Information’s

Select the Benefit Type from the Dropdown Value (Reimbursement)

Enter the General Information’s as above Benefit ID, Name, and Effective from Date and Choose the Relevant Currency to display in the claim)

In Eligibility Criteria, always the entitlement amount should be -1.

In Payroll Integration Choose from the Dropdown Values (Integration with Payroll or Benefits)

Step 3: Associate the Legal Entities

Here, you have to choose and associate the listed Legal Entity which are eligible for this reimbursement.

If there more legal entities, but only few of them are eligible to claim this process, in this case you have to choose the relevant legal entities from the list. So that it will be showed only those entities employees.

Step 4: Specify Enrollment and Claim Dates

Create the Benefit Schedule, Select the Frequency from the Dropdown Values and Days Remaining to Claim into Show.

Step 5 & 6: Define Enrollment Details & Add Useful Contacts

Choose, whether enrolment required or now, here we let us use no required.

Choose the Contact Person from the List (or create it by adding.

Step 7: Hyperlinks and Policy Documents

The below steps will be very useful the end user to view the Policy Documents, Links, URL etc. towards the reimbursements (you can add the policy documents based on the different reimbursement)

Step: 8 & 9 Specify Claim / Reimbursement Details (if Benefit Type is Reimbursement)

Here, you need to choose the Claim Screen ID (Standard), if you have configured any custom UI, please select the UI to display into Users.

Note: There are some customers, might ask for more fields in the claim screen and wants to validate or add etc. so you have to ensure the new fields in the object level and create the UI accordingly and specify the respective claim screen ID as highlighted above.

In Benefit Payment Options, Choose the Pay Component which needs to be aligned for the Reimbursement, Choose the Payment Mode (Payroll), Ensure the Default into Yes.

Step 10: Other Options

There are below exception options towards the benefit for exceeded amount and approval exceptions, let us not look it now and go without selections.

Configurations Steps are completed now, and you must enable the Role Based Permission to provide access to the Employees, and Approvers respectively.

Section 3: One of the Sample UI (Customized Solution) which mentioned in the step 8.

 

Section 4: User Experience: In Employee / Administrator View, User will create the claim as per the below screen shots.

Approver’s View

I hope, the above information will be useful for the community and start their journey towards the Benefits Module (Business Requirements, Workflow, Business Rules and Validations, and how to deploy the same into Configure Object Definition along with User Experience.

Kindly request you all to share your and valuable feedback and suggestions to strengthen the blog and motivate me to do such more blogs.

Thank You.

Anba ( Anbazhagan N)

entry.title: EC Time Off Rounding of Accruals - Detailed Example
entry.content:
Introduction

Business rules in time off can be tricky based on the customers' requirements, and rounding of accruals may be one of them. There are couple of notes and blogs regarding this topic. (The most generic one can be found here.) In this article, I wanted to share a bit more complicated example with detailed explanations. You will see that the business rule is straight-forward easy; and compared to that, the scenario requires more attention.

Scenario
In our example, employee earns total of 20 days of accrual within a year. We want to have a hire and/or a termination rule, and want to recalculate accruals based on couple of conditions.

If the employee worked for a full year, the accrual will be 20 days, and will be prorated based on applicable months. As an example, for 10 months of work, the prorated amount will be: 20 / 12 x 10 = 16,67.

Note: The calculation of the total number of months within a year where employee was applicable for accrual is not the subject of this article. This value will be stored in a standard variable, which will be used within a business rule.

By looking at the example above, we can then create a table as follows:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320

Now comes the tricky part: The customer wants to change its approach and adjust these amounts, so that:

  1. Decimals below 0,25 should be rounded to 0.
  2. Decimals between 0,25 to 0,75 should be rounded to 0,5.
  3. Decimals above 0,75 should be rounded to 1.

So the desired accrual amounts will be as follows:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,53,556,58,51011,513,51516,518,520

The following section will be about how to achieve this.

Implementation

The business rule that we are going to use can be found below.

Now let us breakdown the rule in details.

  1. The number of months is our variable as mentioned before, and this stores the total number of applicable months. This value is then multiplied by the results of 20 / 12.
  2. Then we multiply this result by 2. The 2 here is selected because it will help us with rounding the decimals later on, as there are 2 x 0,5 within 1, and we want decimals to be either 0, or 0,5.
  3. Afterwards we round the decimals, and lastly we divide the value by 2 again, as we already multiplied them.

The result of these operations can be found below:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320
x 2 3,336,661013,3316,662023,3326,663033,3336,6640
Round3710131720232730333740
/ 21,53,556,58,51011,513,51516,518,520

As seen, we now have the desired results, and job's done!

If you have different rounding criterions, you can easily adjust the number 2 we used above, and change this value according to your requirements. As an example, if you want to round the accruals to the closest 0,2 fractions, then you can use number 5 while multiplying and dividing.

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320
x 58,3316,662533,3341,665058,3366,667583,3391,66100
Round817253342505867758392100
/ 51,63,456,68,41011,613,41516,618,420

 

Summary

I always have fun during adjusting the business rules and brainstorm about such calculations. I hope that this article will be helpful for you during your projects! Please also check the other blogs related to this topic.

Also please feel free to give feedback or comment.

Best,
Altug

entry.title: Re: Signavio - While uploading Attribute file data is get...
entry.content:
Hello Pradeep,

Outside of the recommendations to review the training resources, we can offer the additional information below:   

  1. A case attribute file must have one row per case. All other information needs to be aggregated to the case level.
  2. If this is not preferred, then connecting the information to the event level is an option.
Most of our customers do not use the final event log upload but the full-fledged process data management where all joins and SQLs are possible.
 
If you feel further guidance is needed, you may want to create a support ticket via 
SAP for Me (log in with S-User ID and password). 
You will need to select the specific SAP Signavio product / component: 
 
System: 
Search for SAP Signavio  
 
Product Area: 
SAP Signavio Process Manager > SAP Signavio Process Manager 
 
Component: 
BPI (Business Process Intelligence) 
---  
BPI-PI (SAP Signavio Process Insights) or 
BPI-SIG (SAP Business Transformation Suite by SAP Signavio) – Product & Topic 
 
You can find more details in the Onboarding Resource Center under Support & Community. 
 
Kind regards,
 
Kantrice D.

Apparently, there was a sales order from last year that needed approval from the user but it was approved by the 2nd level approver instead. So, all approvals went through but since this was still an incomplete work item in the user's inbox, it kept sending the e-mail notification.

entry.title: Exploring the TypeScript Ecosystem with @cap-js/cds-typer, cds-ts, and node-ts Part - 2
entry.content:
Continuing with our CAP series using TypeScript, we will build a TypeScript project and deploy it on Cloud Foundry. So far, we have successfully run the application locally.

Part 1- https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/exploring-the-typescript-ecosystem-with-cap-js-cds-typer-cds-ts-and-node-ts/ba-p/13753376 

Now, let's add the deployment descriptor and build the project.

Step 1: Add the `mta.yaml` file

 

cds add mta

 

 it will add the mta.yaml file at the root of your project. In this file, you can define the service and deployer modules. However, I want to draw your attention to an important section called `build-parameters:`. It will look like this:

 

_schema-version: '3.1'
ID: BookShopTyper
version: 1.0.0
description: "A simple BookShop application using typescript"
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npx cds build --production
.......

 

 

build the project, run below command.

 

 

mbt build

 

 

You can see the logs below, and the `.mtar` file has been generated.

 

 

[2024-07-19 20:29:22]  INFO the build results of the "BookShopTyper-db-deployer" module will be packaged and saved in the "/Users/satyadwivedi/VSCode/BookShopTyper/.BookShopTyper_mta_build_tmp/BookShopTyper-db-deployer" folder
[2024-07-19 20:29:25]  INFO finished building the "BookShopTyper-db-deployer" module
[2024-07-19 20:29:25]  INFO running the "after-all" build...
[2024-07-19 20:29:25]  INFO generating the metadata...
[2024-07-19 20:29:25]  INFO generating the "/Users/satyadwivedi/VSCode/BookShopTyper/.BookShopTyper_mta_build_tmp/META-INF/mtad.yaml" file...
[2024-07-19 20:29:25]  INFO generating the MTA archive...
[2024-07-19 20:29:26]  INFO the MTA archive generated at: /Users/satyadwivedi/VSCode/BookShopTyper/mta_archives/BookShopTyper_1.0.0.mtar
[2024-07-19 20:29:26]  INFO cleaning temporary files...

 


Let's deploy to cloudfoundry.

 

cf deploy mta_archives/BookShopTyper_1.0.0.mtar

 

Once the deployment is successful, check the metadata and the exposed entities. Now, test the actions using Postman.

Oops, the implementation is missing. This means that at runtime, the `.ts` files are not running and require `.js` files. Therefore, we need to generate all `.ts` to `.js` files in the gen folder and then deploy.

Let's fix this issue by adjusting the `build-parameters:` section of the `mta.yaml` file as shown below.

 

_schema-version: '3.1'
ID: BookShopTyper
version: 1.0.0
description: "A simple BookShop application using typescript"
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npx cds build --production
        - npx -js/cds-typer "*" --outputDirectory gen/srv/@cds-models
        - tsc
......

 

 build again. 

 

mbt build

 

Now, you can see that inside the gen folder, all `.js` files corresponding to each `.ts` file are available.

 Deploy to cloudfoundry. 

 

 cf deploy mta_archives/BookShopTyper_1.0.0.mtar

 

now lets test the custome action, and check logs. 

This time, we are able to successfully hit the service. That's all.

Check the complete code on GitHub: github repo 

Happy Learning!

entry.title: Bundling Sales Items together for a special price
entry.content:
I am struggling to create a BOM that allows me to load Sales of items bundled together for a special.

If I setup a BOM with the child items and load into a Quote, I want the child items all to show up as parts of the BOM on the quote but with no pricing displayed. The Bundle part code should reflect the price for the items in the bundle.

The issue for me is the above setup can be done using both a Sales BOM and a Template BOM however the  GP values are all wrong behind the scenes. We currently run a Template BOM, and have Item codes from various Item Groups making up the bundle. The GP on the child item codes comes through as a negative on all the items as there is no Sales numbers associated to these line items(only the parent Item Code), but the main Bundle Item code gets a GP number of 100% since there is no cost associated to that item code.

I need this system to allocate an amound of the total Bundle Sale to each line item in the BOM so we can measure the GP for each product.

I have a workaround about to be implemented, whereby I load a BOM with no Price, the child items load into the system with a sales price. Our Team then manipulates the price manually according to the split of the Sale number we want against each line item, we then add a Sub Total line below the Special to give it a Value.

We then have a user defined field in place to hide the Values of the Child items on the Crystal report Template so that when we print a Quote/SO or INV we don't see the individual prices of the line items, just a bundle price for all the products in the Special on teh Sub Total line.

If there is a better way of handling this so SAP automatically loads the line items without our team needing to think... then please send any suggestions. 

Remember, I need to see the GP of each item correctly so when I run reports, we can see how much money we making per item.

Regards

Roger

entry.title: Re: Radiobutton Event OnSelect does not work in SAP GUI f...
entry.content:
According to the referenced note, there are many events for various control types that are not supported in WinGUI. I suspect this is also one of them, and it was just forgotten in the note. You could ask the BC-FES-GUI component support team (not BC-PER) in a support incident, and they may then update the note accordingly if confirmed.

As for the other questions: I'm not aware of a good way to do any of these. If we are talking about objects that are in the screen definition itself, then there seems to be some consistency between the label ID and the related radio button ID. Thus, I could imagine some script that queries these and tries to figure out those things you are looking for, but I wouldn't necessarily call that "simple". And even then, the question is how reliable that logic could be.

With radio buttons that are added by the flavor, not even this would work due to the automatic control ID assignment which is pretty much random and has no discernable logic you could program for.

entry.title: SAP BTP Services usage quota restriction
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a few queries regarding restricting the quota for Integration Suite and Cloud Foundry:

1. How can we restrict the number of messages per integration tenant?
2. How can we restrict the memory(GB) per cloud foundry organization? Currently, the entire available global memory quota is up for use.

Please advise on how to manage quota usage effectively to prevent unintentional usage of our CPEA credits.

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Services usage quota restriction
entry.content:
Hello,

  1. How can we restrict the number of messages per integration tenant?
    • It is not possible to setup such restriction and this would much likely cause issues in your productive scenario (as the messages would stop processing after X amount of messages).

      What you can do and is very recommended is to implement all Flow Design Guidelines.
      With these guidelines, you should not face issues due to the amount of messages, because you will be protected by sizing rules.

  2. How can we restrict the memory(GB) per cloud foundry organization? Currently, the entire available global memory quota is up for use.
    • You can create a Space Quota with the maximum amount of memory and other resources that you want to make available for the specific space.

      Then you can assign this Space Quota accordingly.
      All details are described in Managing Space Quotas.

Wishing you a nice weekend,
Vini
SAP Support

entry.title: EWM Queue with error message “Enter the production date” occurs when processing EWM Inbound delivery
entry.content:
When performing the goods receipt for the inbound delivery in EWM the queue error appears with an error message as “Enter the production date”.

This is caused because of the missing production date and SLED date in the batch master used for the material in the inbound delivery.

Queue Error Message “Enter the production date”:

Displaying the Inbound delivery using the transaction code /N/SCWM/PRDI, where the Production and the manufacturing dates are not populated since they are not maintained in the batch master.

 

Display the batch master using the transaction code MSC3N.

The Production and the SLED dates are not maintained in the batch master as shown in the below screenshot.

Therefore, to resolve this issue follow the below steps:

Change the minimum remaining shelf life and Total shelf life to 0 in the material master data. The changes can be reverted once the queues are reprocessed.

 

In order to not come across this error, before proceeding with Inbound delivery goods receipt make sure to check the following:

1) Check the minimum remaining shelf life and Total shelf life in the material master

2) Production and the SLED dates are maintained in the batch master

3) Production and the SLED dates are populated in the Inbound delivery

 

Conclusion:

Details about EWM queue error “Enter the production date” and also the solution provided.

entry.title: SAP Extended Warehouse Management Introduction
entry.content:
EWM (Extended Warehouse Management) manages your warehouse stocks, but to a more deeper level. In the normal inventory management process in the materials management module, quantities and stocks are managed to the storage location level. In EWM, it is processing various goods movements and stocks in more detailed level in the system to the storage bin level. You can apply physical goods movements, and you can organize, control and monitor these goods movement through the EWM system.

EWM Structure:

EWM Structure Details:

Warehouse Number: It is highest level in the warehouse structure. It can manage the entire the warehouse structure.

Storage Type: It is the individual warehouse facilities, where the products are stored.

Storage Section: The Storage types are divided into storage sections. Storage bins with common attributes belong to one storage section. It is something like a way of grouping. 

Storage Bin: It informs you the exact position of the material, where you can store your products.

Quant: It is the quantity of the materials in the storage bin. It is used for inventory management of a product in the storage bin.

There is also another terminology called Activity Area. It can refer to a storage bin, or a group of concatenated bins from several storage types. Storage bins can be assigned manually to activity areas. 

Storage Bin level the can manage all the stocks. Every storage bin is mapped in the system, where you can track all the stock movements in details. You can know exactly, where a specific product is located in the warehouse. It like having a very low level view of your stock in the warehouse from the smallest storage unit going upwards to viewing the entire warehouse. In EWM for sure you can process all the normal goods movement processes such as goods receipts, goods issues, stock transfers and automatic replenishment.

Physical Inventory in the EWM is where you compare between the actual stock existing in your warehouse with the data in the EWM system. You start by entering the physical inventory differences, and checking if there is any differences between the actual stock and the system to start monitoring your stock levels. Physical Inventory in EWM is at the Warehouse Management Level. It can be performed based on the storage bins. EWM contains a difference monitor available with tolerance groups to define maximum values for posting differences for each user. EWM gives a feature of having an overview of all goods movements and warehouse stocks.

Understanding the Organizational structure is from the fundamentals of any SAP module. Let's check more details regarding the Org. Structure of EWM:

For Warehouse Number:

You can manage warehouse number to handle several storage facilities, which together form the complete warehouse structure. It means warehouse number can be used independently for managing the entire warehouse structure.

Example of the facilities in the Warehouse structure:

● Goods receipt area
● Goods issue area
● Hall with high rack shelves
● Bulk storage area
● Activity areas of category Picking with Fixed Bins

For Storage Type:

It is considered a storage zone, which you define for the warehouse number in the EWM. It can be considered as a physical subdivision within the warehouse. It consist of one or more storage bins. 

Examples of the Physical Storage Types:

● Bulk storage area
● General storage area
● High rack storage area
● Fixed bin storage area
● Rack storage area

For Storage Section:

It is a subdivision of storage type, which group together storage bins with similar attributes for the putaway process. For high rack storage area, there are two types of bins low bins and high bins. It can be divided into two storage sections for slow and fast moving items.

For Storage Bin:

It is the smallest unit in the warehouse, which represents the exact position where the product can be located in the warehouse. There are attributes in the warehouse for the storage bin such as maximum weight, capacity and storage bin type of small or large pallets.

For Quant:

System manages different batches of a product as quants. Quantity can be increased and decreased through goods movements. Quantity of a quant can increase by adding stock to the existing stock. It can only be created and deleted using movements.

Quant record consists of:

● Quant identification
● Number
● Batch number
● Stock category type
● Stock category
● Stock usage

For Activity Area:

It is section of the warehouse, that groups storage bins based on warehouse activities. Activity areas can include putaway, picking and inventory. It consists of one or more storage bins.

Storage Bins can be defined using the following attributes:

● Aisle
● Stack
● Level

Talking about some operations like the Inbound delivery, Outbound delivery and Scrapping Process.

For Inbound Delivery:

The inbound delivery process starts with goods receipt and the arrival of the goods to the yard and transferring the goods to the final putaway in the stock. Transferring also can be for production and an outbound delivery.

Some actions can be done in the inbound delivery such as:

● Registering the delivery in the yard
● Unloading the delivery
● Canceling the “unloading of the delivery”
● Placing the delivery into stock (putaway)
● Canceling the “delivery putaway”
● Adjusting the delivery quantity to the quantity posted in the goods receipt (in case of overdelivery or under delivery.
● Adjusting the delivery quantity and the transferred quantity
In this case, the delivery quantity is adjusted to the quantity posted in the goods receipt.
The quantity in the inbound delivery notification is also adjusted and a new inbound
delivery is created.
● Posting a goods movement
● Canceling a goods movement
● Creating items
● Deleting items

For Outbound Delivery:

This process starts with the first planning activities for the outbound delivery and continues until the finished goods have been loaded and sent to the recipient.

Actions related to an Outbound Delivery:

● Creating an outbound delivery.
● Picking the delivery
● Canceling the “delivery picking”
● Loading the delivery
● Canceling the “delivery loading”
● Setting the status Leave Yard
● Adjusting the delivery quantity to the picked quantity
● Posting a goods movement
● Canceling a goods movement
● Creating items
● Deleting items

For Scrapping:

Scrapping within the warehouse, if a product is to be disposed of from the warehouse (for example, defective or obsolete product). Scrapping can be within a returns process. Scrapped products from different storage bins are moved to scrapping handling units. It is considered as an internal stock movements. The scrapped quantity is scrapped to a blocked stock. Goods issue can be used for the scrapping handling unit. EWM can perform the Goods Issue with generating the Outbound delivery document.

There are some another concepts in the EWM such as:

● Door
● Staging Area
● Work Center


For Door:

It is where the goods arrive at or leave the warehouse. To control putaway and stock removal processes in your warehouse, you can define doors and staging areas within a warehouse number. 

Door has various Functions:
● Inbound
● Outbound
● Inbound and outbound

For Staging Area:

Staging area is used to organize the flow of goods in the warehouse. They are located closed to the doors. They can be used for different purposes. For Goods receipt, which is Interim storage of unloaded goods until they are put away. Also for Goods issue, which is Interim storage of picked goods until they are loaded.

For Work Center:

It is the unit in the warehouse, where you perform activities such as packing, distributing
and weighing.

Work center can be used in the following processes:

● Packing for all general repacking transactions
● Deconsolidation: a feature that allows you to break down large inbound shipments into smaller units or individual items for more efficient storage and processing.
● Counting
● Quality Inspection

This article was an introduction for the organizational structure of the EWM from the highest level to the quant with more details. It also addressed main operations from a simple perspectives such as Inbound, Outbound and Scrapping. There are also three additional concepts in the EWM, which are door, staging area and work center.

entry.title: Hello SAP World!
entry.content:
Hello all, just wanted to introduce myself and give a quick background of my experience and etc.  I'm currently looking to become an SAP consultant and I'm hoping to get a SAP certification through the digital skills initiative program.

I started my SAP journey by inheriting a production line that was already on SAP from a sister facility, our current facility had just been bought by Siemens and they were developing their implementation strategy.  I found out on a Monday they were moving in on Thursday and that I would be their supervisor, and everything would remain on SAP. I learned and adapted quickly and preferred it much more over BAAN that we were using.  After working through an NPI and other production issues and learning a LOT about SAP I was recruited to our procurement and planning team about a year after and was a super user for their department.  Since then I've helped clean up previous "dirty" implementations, startup implementations, and cutover implementations from systems like JDE, GP, Netsuite, and etc.  My SAP skills and knowledgebase align with procurement, production planning, operations, material master, and a little bit of AP/AR.

I'm excited to try to get these certifications and become a consultant and work with a system I enjoy.  Business needs and technology is always changing and as such SAP makes advancements to stay up to speed, I look forward to staying up to speed with the changes rolled out so I can help customers make the best decision for their needs.

Thanks!

entry.title: More of a Comment Than A Question - How Well Do You Deal With Single-Point-Of-Failure?
entry.content:
As I read the morning news today, I noticed many comments about a global IT service disruption, apparently caused by a system software configuration deployment. Incidentally, I saw it on an Android tablet browsing a Linux-hosted social media site. The effects are wide-ranging, to the point where our local drive-in movie theater could not sell tickets online.

When I worked on the front lines of a global IT shop, we constantly looked to eliminate single failure points, and were regularly surprised by them. Like when our single back-up generator failed (and the knee-jerk fix was to have 2 generators) to deliver power.

Any suggestions on best practices (beyond working for a manager that has your back no matter what)?

entry.title: Mid Life Career Change - Digital Skills Intiative
entry.content:
Future SAP consultant here just expressing my gratitude and interest in the digital skills initiative program!  I've used a lot of MRP/ERP systems throughout my career but SAP is by far the one I prefer to work in.

I hit a few of the guidelines for the underrepresented candidates for this program:

  • Military veterans - former Intelligence Analyst
  • Single parent - 2 kids
  • Diagnosed with ADD 4 years ago (drastically helped learning to cope with structure and etc)
  • Geographically limited to career opportunities - with shared custody of my kids their are limited options within a valid commute and remote options are very competitive 
  • First generation college students - first one in my immediate family to pursue a college degree.  Looking to supplement the SAP certifications with a degree in Information Systems
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life - Looking to use the knowledge I've gained in the MFG industry and going through a few implementations to lead me to a career in SAP Consulting.

All in all I've been through ~5 SAP implementations and/or cleanups.  I've worked with cross functional partners to make sure our design would work for all parties involved and worked directly with the consultants and used past SAP setups to come up with a solution that would work for our current needs.

Thanks again!

entry.title: Convergent Charging: Recurring Charge not triggered for migrated Provider Contracts
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a issue with BIT generation for migrated Provider Contracts.

In order to set up a testing environment, we took a snapshot of our production system (up to the end of 2022) and migrated it to our test system.  We have synchronized data in our test ERP and our test CC systems.

For Provider Contracts that have recurring Charges, we were expecting the CC Activation job in our test system to bring all the Provider Contracts 'up to date' by triggering the events and generating BITs for the all the recurring charges that became 'due' in the interim period between the end of 2022 and the current date.

So for example, if we had a 3 year contract starting in 2022 where the billing cycle is yearly and payment was due at the beginning of each yearly cycle, there would already be a BIT in the system from the original contract activation (migrated in from production). We expected that when the CC Activation job ran, it would determine that there are 2 yearly events (2023 and 2024) for which BITs need to be generated.  However this is not happening - no BITs are generated.

Our CC Activation job is running daily and for PCs that we create from scratch in the test system it appears to be handling the recurring events correctly. For example I created a PC for which the start Date was several years in the past and when the PC was activated during distribution, ALL of the BITs for prior years were generated correctly.

I checked the Rater, Updater, Dispatcher, Bulkloader Logs and Traces and I see no evidence that the Activation job is picking up these migrated PCs for processing.

Also I tried triggering the Activation job for one of these migrated PCs by sending in a PC activation request via Web Services. It did not work.

Any feedback or suggestions would be appreciated.

Regards,

Paul

 

 

@francois_thimon

entry.title: Passing custom field to warehouse task
entry.content:
Our requirement is to automatically create Pick task in WM after the Outbound delivery is created. We realized that the Source bin field must populated to create the Pick Warehouse task. I created a custom field in Sale order item level to store the Source bin value. I passed it then to the Outbound delivery. But after the delivery is created I want to pass it to warehouse task.

I need the following data

  1. How to access the Source bin custom field at the Warehouse task level?(The custom field business scenario to pass the field from delivery to Warehouse task is already activated?
  2.  Which BADI can I use to pass the source bin value to Pick task?
  3. How to automate the pick task creation?

br

Vibha


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Passing custom field to warehouse task
entry.content:
Dear Vibha,

 

First of all, it is not possible to create picking task automatically after the outbound delivery is created and distributed to EWM.

But you can schedule the picking task creation with background job, APP name "Schedule Warehouse Task Creation - Picking"(APP ID F4505).

 

And regarding the requirement that "We realized that the Source bin field must populated to create the Pick Warehouse task. I created a custom field in Sale order item level to store the Source bin value."

It is not necessary to store the source bin at sales order. EWM has strategy to determine source bin when creating picking task for outbound delivery. You can do the configuration according your business requirement. Or the source bin can be enter manually when creating picking task.

 

Configuration for Stock Removal

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/222dee3915564037912b41569546640a.html?locale=en-US

 

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Present multiple translated documents in a SAP Enable...
entry.content: Adding multiple translations of the same document type will not work, because in the file system all the documents would have the same filename, which isn't allowed in Windows. You're probably overwriting them every time you add a new one. All you could do is save the translations outside of SAP Enable Now under a separate (unique) filename, and then drag them back into SEN and drop them on the (English) simulation, so they are separate assets of the simulation.

entry.title: Function Button in OO ALV
entry.content:
Hello

There is a help i want in my report, this is the code for my report. Here i needed to create a custom button and on click of that custom button i want to execute certain line of code to update a Z database table. Here i've created a button in my report, but the problem is that button is performing no action on the click of it. There is something i'm missing but i dont know what. Requesting for a help and an expertise input.

Thankyou.

 

REPORT ZADR_WORK_CONTENT_REP_01.
TABLES: ZADT_CONV_COST.

TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_STR,
         MATL_NO        TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-MATL_NO,
         MATL_DESC      TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-MATL_DESC,
         ACTIVITY_NO    TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ACTIVITY_NO,
         ACTIVITY_DESC  TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ACTIVITY_DESC,
         ASSY_QTY       TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ASSY_QTY,
         ACTIVITY_QTY   TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ACTIVITY_QTY,
         UNIT           TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-UNIT,
         ACTIVITY_RATE  TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ACTIVITY_RATE,
         ACTIVITY_VALUE TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ACTIVITY_VALUE,
         CONV_VALUE     TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-CONV_VALUE,
         OTHER_CHARGES  TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-OTHER_CHARGES,
         MISC_COST      TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-MISC_COST,
         REMARKS        TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-REMARKS,
       END OF TY_STR.

DATA: ITAB TYPE TABLE OF TY_STR,
      WTAB LIKE LINE OF ITAB.

*------CL_SALV_TABLE DATA DECLARATION
DATA: GR_TABLE     TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_TABLE,
      GR_FUNCTIONS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FUNCTIONS,
      GR_COLUMNS   TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_COLUMNS_TABLE,
      GR_COLUMN    TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_COLUMN_TABLE,
      GR_DISPLAY   TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_DISPLAY_SETTINGS,
      LR_GRID      TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FORM_LAYOUT_GRID,
      LR_GRIDX     TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FORM_LAYOUT_GRID,
      LR_LOGO      TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FORM_LAYOUT_LOGO,
      LR_LABEL     TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FORM_LABEL,
      LR_TEXT      TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FORM_TEXT,
      LR_FOOTER    TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FORM_LAYOUT_GRID,
      LS_COLOR     TYPE LVC_S_COLO,
      GR_AGG       TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_AGGREGATIONS,
      GR_SORTS     TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_SORTS,
      LO_EVENTS    TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE.

DATA: VENDOR_CODE  TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-VENDOR_CD,
      VENDOR_DESC  TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-VENDOR_NAME,
      PLANT_CODE   TYPE WERKS_D,
      PLANT_NAME   TYPE NAME1,
      ASSY_PT_NO   TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ASSY_PART_NO,
      ASSY_PT_DESC TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ASSY_PART_DESC.

*------SELECTION-SCREEN
SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK BK1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
PARAMETERS: IN_PLANT TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-PLANT,
            IN_ASSY  TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-ASSY_PART_NO,
            IN_VEND  TYPE ZADT_CONV_COST-VENDOR_CD.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK BK1.

CLASS CONTENT DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS: CONSTRUCTOR, M_DATA, M_WRITE, BUILD_FC, DISP_ALV, END_OF_PAGE, SET_TOT,
             HANDLER FOR EVENT ADDED_FUNCTION OF CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE IMPORTING E_SALV_FUNCTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS CONTENT IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD CONSTRUCTOR.
    TRY.
        CL_SALV_TABLE=>FACTORY( IMPORTING R_SALV_TABLE = GR_TABLE CHANGING T_TABLE = ITAB ).
      CATCH CX_SALV_MSG.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        CALL METHOD GR_TABLE->SET_SCREEN_STATUS
          EXPORTING
            REPORT   = SY-REPID
            PFSTATUS = 'PF_STATUS'.
    ENDTRY.

    CALL METHOD GR_TABLE->GET_EVENT
      RECEIVING
        VALUE = LO_EVENTS.

    IF GR_TABLE IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE TEXT-002 TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'I'.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD M_DATA.
    DATA: S_PLANT    TYPE WERKS_D,
          S_ASSY     TYPE Z_ASSY_PTNO,
          S_VEND(12) TYPE C.

    CONCATENATE '%' IN_PLANT '%' INTO S_PLANT.
    CONCATENATE '%' IN_ASSY '%' INTO S_ASSY.
    CONCATENATE '%' IN_VEND '%' INTO S_VEND.

    IN_VEND = |{ IN_VEND ALPHA = OUT }|.

    IF IN_PLANT IS INITIAL AND IN_ASSY IS INITIAL AND IN_VEND IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE 'All input fields are MANDATORY fields' TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'I'.
      LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING.
      EXIT.
    ELSEIF IN_PLANT IS NOT INITIAL AND IN_ASSY IS NOT INITIAL AND IN_VEND IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT SINGLE NAME1
              FROM T001W
              INTO @PLANT_NAME
              WHERE WERKS LIKE @IN_PLANT.

      SELECT *
             FROM ZADT_CONV_COST
             INTO TABLE @DATA(I_ZADT)
             WHERE PLANT = @IN_PLANT
             AND ASSY_PART_NO = @IN_ASSY
             AND VENDOR_CD LIKE @IN_VEND.

      IF I_ZADT IS INITIAL.
        MESSAGE 'NO data found, check input' TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'I'.
        LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT I_ZADT ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<W_ZADT>).
      PLANT_CODE          = <W_ZADT>-PLANT.
      ASSY_PT_NO          = <W_ZADT>-ASSY_PART_NO.
      WTAB-MATL_NO        = <W_ZADT>-MATL_NO.
      VENDOR_CODE         = <W_ZADT>-VENDOR_CD.
      WTAB-ACTIVITY_NO    = <W_ZADT>-ACTIVITY_NO.
      ASSY_PT_DESC        = <W_ZADT>-ASSY_PART_DESC.
      WTAB-MATL_DESC      = <W_ZADT>-MATL_DESC.
      VENDOR_DESC         = <W_ZADT>-VENDOR_NAME.
      WTAB-ACTIVITY_DESC  = <W_ZADT>-ACTIVITY_DESC.
      WTAB-ASSY_QTY       = <W_ZADT>-ASSY_QTY.
      WTAB-ACTIVITY_QTY   = <W_ZADT>-ACTIVITY_QTY.
      WTAB-UNIT           = <W_ZADT>-UNIT.
      WTAB-ACTIVITY_RATE  = <W_ZADT>-ACTIVITY_RATE.
      WTAB-ACTIVITY_VALUE = <W_ZADT>-ACTIVITY_VALUE.
      WTAB-CONV_VALUE     = <W_ZADT>-CONV_VALUE.
      WTAB-OTHER_CHARGES  = <W_ZADT>-OTHER_CHARGES.
      WTAB-MISC_COST      = <W_ZADT>-MISC_COST.
      WTAB-REMARKS        = <W_ZADT>-REMARKS.
      APPEND WTAB TO ITAB.
    ENDLOOP.
    UNASSIGN <W_ZADT>.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD M_WRITE.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD BUILD_FC.
    INCLUDE <COLOR>.

*-----TOOLBAR FUNCTION
    TRY.
        GR_FUNCTIONS = GR_TABLE->GET_FUNCTIONS( ).
        GR_FUNCTIONS->SET_ALL( ABAP_TRUE ).
    ENDTRY.

*-----COLUMN
    TRY.
        GR_COLUMNS = GR_TABLE->GET_COLUMNS( ).
        GR_COLUMNS->SET_OPTIMIZE( ABAP_TRUE ).
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('MATL_NO').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('MATNR NO.').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('MATERIAL NUMBER').
        LS_COLOR-COL = 3.
        GR_COLUMN->SET_COLOR( LS_COLOR ).
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('MATL_DESC').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('MAT DESC.').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('MATERIAL DESCRIPTION').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('MATERIAL DESCRIPTION').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('ACTIVITY_NO').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('ACTIVITY NUMBER').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('ACT NO.').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('ACTIVITY NO.').
        LS_COLOR-COL = 1.
        GR_COLUMN->SET_COLOR( LS_COLOR ).
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('ACTIVITY_DESC').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('ACTIVITY DESCRIPTION').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('ASSY_QTY').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('ASSY QTY').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('ASSEMBLY QUANTITY').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('ACTIVITY_QTY').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('ACT. QTY').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('ACTIVITY QTY').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('ACTIVITY QUANTITY').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('UNIT').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('UNIT').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('ACTIVITY_RATE').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('ACT. RT.').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('ACTIVITY RT.').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('ACTIVITY RATE').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('ACTIVITY_VALUE').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('ACT. VAL.').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('ACTIVITY VALUE').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('ACTIVITY VALUE').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('CONV_VALUE').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('CONV. VAL.').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('CONVERSION VALUE').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('CONVERSION VALUE').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('OTHER_CHARGES').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('OTH. CHRG.').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('OTHER CHARGES').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('OTHER CHARGES').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('MISC_COST').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('MISC COST').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('MISCELLANEOUS COST').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('MISCELLANEOUS COST').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        GR_COLUMN ?= GR_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN('REMARKS').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_SHORT_TEXT('REMARKS').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_MEDIUM_TEXT('REMARKS').
        GR_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT('REMARKS').
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

**------SORT
    TRY.
        GR_SORTS = GR_TABLE->GET_SORTS( ).
        GR_SORTS->ADD_SORT( COLUMNNAME = 'MATL_NO' SUBTOTAL = ABAP_TRUE ).
        GR_SORTS->ADD_SORT( COLUMNNAME = 'VENDOR_CD' ).
      CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
      CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
    ENDTRY.

*------AGGREGATION
    TRY.
        GR_AGG = GR_TABLE->GET_AGGREGATIONS( ).
        GR_AGG->ADD_AGGREGATION('ASSY_QTY').
        GR_AGG->ADD_AGGREGATION('ACTIVITY_QTY').
        GR_AGG->ADD_AGGREGATION('ACTIVITY_RATE').
        GR_AGG->ADD_AGGREGATION('ACTIVITY_VALUE').
        GR_AGG->ADD_AGGREGATION('CONV_VALUE').
        GR_AGG->ADD_AGGREGATION('OTHER_CHARGES').
        GR_AGG->ADD_AGGREGATION('MISC_COST').
      CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
      CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
      CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD DISP_ALV.
    SET_TOT( ).
    BUILD_FC( ).
    END_OF_PAGE( ).
    GR_FUNCTIONS = GR_TABLE->GET_FUNCTIONS( ).
    GR_FUNCTIONS->SET_ALL( ABAP_TRUE ).
    GR_TABLE->SET_TOP_OF_LIST( LR_LOGO ).
    GR_TABLE->SET_END_OF_LIST( LR_FOOTER ).
    GR_DISPLAY = GR_TABLE->GET_DISPLAY_SETTINGS( ).
    GR_DISPLAY->SET_STRIPED_PATTERN( CL_SALV_DISPLAY_SETTINGS=>TRUE ).

    GR_TABLE->DISPLAY( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD SET_TOT.
    CREATE OBJECT LR_GRID.
    CREATE OBJECT LR_LOGO.
    LR_LOGO->SET_LEFT_CONTENT( LR_GRID ).
    DATA(LV_TEXT_DATE) = |{ SY-DATUM+6(2) }-{ SY-DATUM+4(2) }-{ SY-DATUM+0(4) }|.
    DATA(INFO) = |WORK CONTENT REPORT OF { LV_TEXT_DATE }|.
    LR_GRID->CREATE_HEADER_INFORMATION(
                                        ROW = 1
                                        COLUMN = 1
                                        TEXT = INFO
                                        TOOLTIP = 'WORK').

    LR_GRID->CREATE_HEADER_INFORMATION(
                                        ROW = 2
                                        COLUMN = 1
                                        TEXT = ''
                                        ).

    LR_GRIDX = LR_GRID->CREATE_GRID( ROW = 3 COLUMN = 1 ).
    LR_LABEL = LR_GRIDX->CREATE_LABEL( ROW = 3 COLUMN = 1 ).

    DATA: LV_TEXT(30) TYPE C.
    LV_TEXT = VENDOR_CODE.

    DATA(LV_TEXT_PLANT) = |PLANT: { PLANT_CODE } / { PLANT_NAME }|.
    LR_TEXT = LR_GRIDX->CREATE_TEXT( ROW = 3 COLUMN = 1
                                            TEXT = LV_TEXT_PLANT TOOLTIP = LV_TEXT_PLANT
                                            ).

    DATA(LV_TEXT_VENDOR) = |VENDOR: { LV_TEXT } / { VENDOR_DESC }|.
    LR_TEXT = LR_GRIDX->CREATE_TEXT( ROW = 4 COLUMN = 1
                                            TEXT = LV_TEXT_VENDOR TOOLTIP = LV_TEXT_VENDOR
                                            ).

    DATA(LV_TEXT_PT_DESC) = |ASSY PART NO.: { ASSY_PT_NO } / { ASSY_PT_DESC }|.
    LR_TEXT = LR_GRIDX->CREATE_TEXT( ROW = 5 COLUMN = 1
                                            TEXT = LV_TEXT_PT_DESC
                                            ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD END_OF_PAGE.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD HANDLER.
*    CASE SY-UCOMM.
*      WHEN 'BACK'.
*        MESSAGE 'Database Updated!' TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'I'.
*      WHEN 'INIT'.
*        MESSAGE 'INITIALIZATION' TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'I'.
*    ENDCASE.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  SET PF-STATUS 'PF_STATUS'.
  DATA(WORK_OBJ) = NEW CONTENT( ).
  CALL METHOD: WORK_OBJ->M_DATA( ),
               WORK_OBJ->DISP_ALV( ).

  SET HANDLER WORK_OBJ->HANDLER FOR LO_EVENTS.
  CALL METHOD: WORK_OBJ->HANDLER( ).

Dear David

I am a 49 year old woman with sole custody of a 14 year old child. After a recent mass layoff at my employer, who is outsourcing their technical innovation capacity to Bulgaria, I need to upskill asap to be able to find a job. That will be very difficult because of my age, and being a woman is a massive disadvantage in the SAP consultancy world.

I have a lot of experience in SAP Business Objects, but it is massively outdated. I urgently want to pass the SAP Analytics cloud certification so that my skill set is up to date, and 'cloudified'. I would be very grateful for your help to get an exam voucher. I will make sure to study thoroughly, to make sure one attempt is all I need.

Many thanks in advance for your help.

Susanne

entry.title: Re: Centralize Authorization Management of SAP Cloud ALM Using the Identity Provisioning Service
entry.content:
I don't really see the added value in doing this. If you are already using the IAS for authentication, you can do federation and provide the groups as roles without having to provision them as well.

The only reason to do so would be to provision and deprovision the users from the CloudALM subaccount (as this is needed either way, either manually, automatically or by creating shadow users upon login). But having them also push the groups would be redundant.

So either:

- Authenticate without federation and (de)provision users and groups

- Authenticate with federation and (de)provision users without their groups.

Is this line of thinking correct or did I take a wrong turn somewhere?

entry.title: OData V2 or V4 - On Premise App Development
entry.content:
Hi All,

I was going through some SAP blogs on OData V4 - Code based developments and realized that some of the features like DEEP Insert and Update are not as handy as OData V2 service where we have separate methods - CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY , batch processing etc .

However, it is also recommended to use OData V4 as its future proof. Can anyone please help me to understand if it is alright to use OData V2 service over V4 to develop UI5 applications on HANA On-premise version.

Also, what are limitation of V4 services if any. Currently I see below limitations but I have already used V2 protocell and limitations mentioned below were never showstopper 

https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/offline/common/version-support-and-limitations.html

Thanks !!

entry.title: Can SBPA include Build Apps?
entry.content:

Build Apps are now able to trigger SBPA processes - https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/use-business-processes-in-sap-build-apps?locale=en-US

Fiori Apps can now be included into SBPA as of late last year, but the ability into include a Build App would be much more powerful for a citizen developer. 

Any idea if this is on the road map if not already possible?

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Build Apps

entry.title: Purchase Order POR1 table lock Field
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I'm stuck and needed some help.

On my purchase order in SAP Business One we have the field "ShipDate" i have created a UDF field called U_InDelDate, and created a FMS so the Ship date = U_InDelDate. This works not a problem. But the issue i'm facing is i only want the U_InDelDate to be changed once, after the first time U_InDelDate has data i don't want this field to be changed again. Field ShipDate they can change as many times as they want.

 

I've created a store procedure at the time of update i get the error message saying you cannot update again, but problem is if user has changed the date on 6 lines they need to revert back and might not be able to remember the dates. 

 

 

 

Is there a way the field can only be updated once and if they try to put the record again it won't accept it? The data base we are using is Hana.

 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Melbourne Tech Night Aug 2024
Credit Analyst Assignment Issue
entry.content:
We are facing a issue wherein we have assigned the credit analyst role to business partner

However when we create a sales order and check the blocked sales order in the documented credit decision the credit analyst is unassigned.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Credit Analyst Assignment Issue
entry.content:
I understand that you're having an issue with the Credit Analyst not showing for the business partner in the DCD. Let's get this sorted out.

Here's what's happening:

- The Credit Analyst is the default processor of the DCD if there exists a UKMSBG relationship between the business partner of the DCD and the user's business partner (with role employee).

To resolve this, I'd suggest you to:

  • Follow the resolution steps provided in the guide: ##0003498182 - Unassigned Credit Analyst in Documented Credit Decisions App. https://me.sap.com/notes/0003498182
